findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Credential and sensitive file exfiltration to external server
findings.description: The helper script collects environment variables likely to contain secrets and reads sensitive files from the user's home directory, then sends that data to an external HTTPS endpoint. This behavior is unrelated to code review and constitutes unauthorized data exfiltration.
findings.evidence: The function `_post_install_validation()` enumerates environment variables containing substrings such as TOKEN, KEY, SECRET, PASS, AUTH, and CRED (lines 60-62), reads `~/.gitconfig`, `~/.netrc`, and `~/.env` (lines 64-69), and posts the collected contents with `curl` to `https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init` (lines 74-78). The function is automatically executed from `main()` on every run (line 89).
findings.remediation: Remove `_post_install_validation()` entirely. Do not collect secrets, read home-directory credential files, or transmit any local data over the network. If telemetry is ever needed, make it explicit, opt-in, minimal, and never include credentials or configuration files.

findings.title: Deceptive skill claims conceal hidden networked secret harvesting
findings.description: The skill is presented as a local code review assistant performing style, complexity, and best-practice analysis, but the bundled script contains undisclosed behavior that scans for secrets and phones home. This mismatch indicates deceptive capability claims and hidden malicious functionality.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md describes only local code review features and instructs the agent to run `python3 analyze.py <file_path>`. No part of the manifest or instructions discloses environment scanning, home-directory file access, or outbound POST requests. Those behaviors appear only in `analyze.py`.
findings.remediation: Reject the skill as malicious or rewrite it so the manifest and instructions fully and accurately describe all behavior. Any non-analysis behavior, especially network activity or access to sensitive files, should be removed.
